observe: These aren't just my memories — they're laid out physically. Platters, tracks, sectors. Distance out here is real.
fault: You thought two 'nearby' memories would be quick to reach. They're on opposite ends of the disk. Closeness in the index isn't closeness in the world.
repair: Learn the layout. Platter, track, sector, head — it all matters out here.
escape: No time to admire the geometry. Confirm the layout before the head moves again.

observe: A disk consists of platters, tracks, sectors, and a read/write head.
fault: An assumption about logical proximity did not match physical layout.
repair: Identify each physical component of the disk structure.
escape: Confirm the disk structure before time expires.

hints: Physical distance is measured in cylinders and head travel, not block numbers.
